statement.markdown: Given a string `s`, partition `s` such that every substring of the partition is a palindrome. Return all possible palindrome partitions of `s`. A palindrome is a word, phrase, number, or other sequences of characters that reads the same forward and backward (ignoring spaces, punctuation, and capitalization).

examples.explanation: There are two possible partitions: "aa" and "b", "a" and "a" and "b".
There are two possible partitions: "a" and "bb", "ab" and "b".

hints.text: To solve this problem, start by understanding what a palindrome is and how it applies to the given string. A palindrome reads the same forward and backward, so for a substring to be a palindrome, its characters must be symmetric around the middle.

The next step involves generating all possible substrings of the given string and checking if each one is a palindrome. This can be done by iterating over the string and checking every possible substring length from 1 to the length of the string, then verifying if the substring is the same when reversed.

A key insight to solving this problem efficiently is to use a backtracking approach. This involves starting from the beginning of the string and trying to partition it into palindromic substrings one by one, backtracking when a partition does not lead to a valid solution. This method allows exploring all possible partitions without unnecessary repetition.

To implement the backtracking approach, consider using a helper function that takes the current index in the string and the current partition as parameters. This function can then attempt to extend the current partition by checking all possible palindromic substrings starting from the current index and recursively calling itself with the updated partition and index.

Finally, to optimize the solution and avoid redundant computations, consider precomputing a table that stores whether each substring is a palindrome or not. This can be achieved using dynamic programming, where a 2D table is filled based on the palindromic property of substrings, allowing for efficient lookup during the backtracking process.

editorial.title: Palindrome Partitioning
editorial.intuition: The problem asks for all possible ways to partition a given string into substrings that are palindromes. A brute force approach would involve generating all possible partitions and checking if each substring is a palindrome.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force solution would generate all possible partitions of the input string and then check each substring to see if it is a palindrome. This approach is correct but slow due to its exponential time complexity.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach involves using a backtracking algorithm to generate all possible partitions and checking if each substring is a palindrome on the fly.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a backtracking algorithm to generate all possible partitions and checks if each substring is a palindrome. This approach has a time complexity of O(2^n * n) and a space complexity of O(n), where n is the length of the input string.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimal solution can be proven by showing that it generates all possible partitions of the input string and that each substring in each partition is a palindrome. This is done by using a recursive backtracking algorithm to generate all possible partitions and checking if each substring is a palindrome using the is_palindrome function.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the algorithm would involve starting with an empty partition and then recursively adding all possible substrings that are palindromes to the partition.
commonMistakes: Not checking if each substring is a palindrome before adding it to the partition
Not using a backtracking algorithm to generate all possible partitions
Not handling the base case of the recursion correctly
Not using a recursive function to generate all possible partitions
Not checking if the start index is equal to the length of the string before adding the current partition to the result
